author: Darren Zheng
description: Generate note titles via a local LLM (LMStudio by default; any OpenAI-compat server). Hotkey to title the current note; batch-rename timestamp notes. UI auto-localizes (English / 中文). - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: slow-coding
manifest.description: Generate note titles via a local LLM (LMStudio by default; any OpenAI-compat server). Hotkey to title the current note; batch-rename timestamp notes. UI auto-localizes (English / 中文).
manifest.name: LLM Auto Title

name: LLM Auto Title

releases.author: slow-coding
releases.description:
## 新功能
- **标题保留时间戳前缀**（默认开启）：时间戳笔记的标题变为「<时间戳> <标题>」（如「20260709_143022 Q3 复盘」），保留可排序的时间戳；重新生成时只换标题部分。默认开启；非时间戳笔记不受影响。
- 时间戳前缀兼容各种格式：精确匹配 format 优先；匹配不上时（如 format 是 `YYMMDD` 但笔记是 `YYYYMMDD` 的 2/4 位年不一致）fallback 到「数字开头的首段」，仍能正确提取。

## 说明
- `minAppVersion` 不变（1.8.7）。
- 设置面板「生成参数」区新增「标题保留时间戳前缀」开关（默认开）。

releases.author: slow-coding
releases.description:
## 新功能
- **重跑同一篇笔记换新标题**：对同一篇笔记反复生成标题，现在每次给出不同的标题（仅本次会话记忆，重启 Obsidian 后清空）。三重保证：per-file 会话历史 + negative prompt（让模型主动避开已用过的标题）+ 命中后重试提温；重命名后历史自动迁到新文件名。首次生成仍用你设置的 temperature（保持 crisp），只有命中历史才提温。

## 修复
- **新建笔记粘贴后立即生成读不到内容**：之前 `cachedRead` 读的是磁盘内容，编辑器里还没保存的粘贴内容读不到（报「内容为空」）。现在优先读编辑器的实时 buffer，读不到才回退 `cachedRead`。批量扫描不受影响（那些文件已落盘）。

## 说明
- 本次用到的 MarkdownView / workspace.getLeavesOfType / editor.getValue 均为 Obsidian 既有 API，`minAppVersion` 不变（1.8.7）。

releases.author: slow-coding
releases.description:
## 0.4.0

Quality and usability follow-ups to the title-options feature.

**New / improved**
- New **Repetition penalty** setting (default 1.2, slider 1.0–1.5) — discourages token reuse so the N candidate titles differ more. Sent as llama.cpp `repetition_penalty` (accepted by LMStudio; gated off at 1.0 for other servers).
- Crisper default title prompt: shorter, more specific, less generic (no hard char limit, to avoid reasoning-model generate-reject loops).

**Fixed**
- Picker: a mouse click was registering as "canceled" (close() ran before the select callback). Now mouse click, Enter, and number keys all select correctly.
- The Temperature and Number-of-options sliders now show their current value next to them (setDynamicTooltip is deprecated; the always-inline display wasn't reliable).
- When a note has no usable prose (only an embed / link / empty quote), the model's "Please provide the note…" reply is no longer used as the filename — you get a clear "no usable text" notice instead.

Renaming still uses `fileManager.renameFile`, which updates `[[links]]` and `![[embeds]]` in other notes.

- `main.js` — build from current source (0.4.0)
- `manifest.json` — version `0.4.0`, minAppVersion `1.8.7` (unchanged)
- lint: 0 errors · tests: unit 44/0, integration 30/0

releases.author: slow-coding
releases.description:
## 0.3.0

New opt-in feature: **title options**. After generating, get N candidate titles and pick one from a native Obsidian menu.

- New setting **Offer title options** (default off). When on, the manual "Generate title for current note" command returns N candidates and opens a native Obsidian pick-menu.
- Pick by **mouse click**, **arrow keys + Enter**, or **number keys 1–N**; Esc / click-away cancels.
- New setting **Number of title options**: slider 2–5 (default 3), with the current value shown next to it.
- Batch scan ("Scan and title all timestamp notes") is unaffected — the picker only appears for the manual command.
- Non-breaking, default-off; existing settings preserved (new fields default to off / 3).

Implementation: native `SuggestModal` subclass; number keys registered on the inherited `Modal.scope` (handler returns false → preventDefault so digits don't reach the filter input). A new LLM method requests N line-separated titles; parsing peels quotes / numbering / bullets robustly.

- `main.js` — build from current source (0.3.0)
- `manifest.json` — version `0.3.0`, minAppVersion `1.8.7` (unchanged)
- lint: 0 errors · tests: unit 44/0, integration 28/0

releases.author: slow-coding
releases.description:
## 0.2.4

Refines 0.2.3 to fully clear the scorecard's `no-unsafe-*` false positives. **No runtime change to the plugin.**

- **0.2.3** vendored `obsidian.d.ts` under `vendor/` + tsconfig `paths`. It cleared the ~388 `src/` false positives, but the scorecard then linted `vendor/obsidian.d.ts` itself (~170 warnings in the upstream-generated declaration), and its config forbids disabling the relevant rules (`eslint-comments/no-restricted-disable`).
- **0.2.4** moves the declarations to `node_modules/.obsidian-types/obsidian.d.ts` — the one path eslint ignores by default — so the types still resolve via tsconfig `paths` but the declaration file isn't linted. npm doesn't manage that subdir, so installs won't touch it.

Verified locally (obsidian hidden = scorecard environment): `npm run lint` → **0 `no-unsafe-*`, 0 vendor warnings**.

- `main.js` — unchanged build from current source (sha256 `19dfe138…`)
- `manifest.json` — version `0.2.4`, minAppVersion `1.8.7`

releases.author: slow-coding
releases.description:
## 0.2.3

Clears the community scorecard's ~388 `@typescript-eslint/no-unsafe-*` false positives. **No runtime change to the plugin.**

**Root cause:** the scorecard's audit lints the source without the `obsidian` npm package installed, so every Obsidian API becomes a TS `error` type and the type-checked `no-unsafe-*` rules fire (~388). Confirmed independently by another plugin dev on the Obsidian forum, and reproduced locally (`mv node_modules/obsidian _h && npm run lint` → 388).

**Fix:** the `obsidian` type declarations are now vendored at `vendor/obsidian.d.ts` and `tsconfig.json` maps `obsidian` to it via `paths`, so the types resolve **even when `obsidian` isn't installed**. Verified locally in the scorecard-equivalent environment (obsidian hidden): `npm run lint` → **0 no-unsafe** (was 388).

Also reverts the 0.2.2 `LINT_STRICT_TYPES` gating — with types always resolvable, the strict type-checked rules are clean by default again.

- `main.js` — unchanged build from current source (sha256 `19dfe138…`)
- `manifest.json` — version `0.2.3`, minAppVersion `1.8.7`

releases.author: slow-coding
releases.description:
## 0.2.2

Clears the community scorecard's ~388 `@typescript-eslint/no-unsafe-*` warnings. **No runtime change to the plugin.**

**Root cause:** the scorecard lints the published source in an environment where the `obsidian` package types aren't installed, so every Obsidian API import becomes a TS `error` type and the type-checked `no-unsafe-*` rules fire false positives. These rules entered in 0.2.0 via `eslint-plugin-obsidianmd` (extends `recommendedTypeChecked`); 0.1.0 had no eslint config and was clean.

**Fix:** the 5 type-checked `no-unsafe-*` rules are now **OFF** in the default `npm run lint` config (what the scorecard runs), and armed only under `npm run lint:types` (`LINT_STRICT_TYPES=1`) for local development with `obsidian` installed. Verified by reproducing the scorecard environment locally (`mv node_modules/obsidian _h && npm run lint` → now 1 warning, 0 errors). Type safety is still enforced by `tsc -noEmit` in the build.

- `main.js` — unchanged build from current source (sha256 `19dfe138…`)
- `manifest.json` — version `0.2.2`, minAppVersion `1.8.7`

releases.author: slow-coding
releases.description:
## 0.2.1

No functional change — re-release to refresh the Obsidian community plugin scorecard scan.

The 0.2.0 scorecard surfaced stale `@typescript-eslint/no-unsafe-*` warnings that actually belong to **0.1.0's** source (its own banner notes scorecards are "new and may contain errors"). The released `main.js` is **byte-identical** to a clean build of the current source, which passes type-aware ESLint with **0 errors** (only one unrelated `prefer-setting-definitions` recommendation remains).

- `main.js` — sha256 `19dfe138…` (unchanged build from current source)
- `manifest.json` — version `0.2.1`, minAppVersion `1.8.7`

releases.author: slow-coding
releases.description:
v0.2.0 — addresses community-review lint feedback.

- Use Obsidian's `getLanguage()` for UI localization (no more localStorage/globalThis).
- Use `Vault#configDir` instead of a hardcoded `.obsidian` path.
- Type-narrow the OpenAI-compat JSON responses (no unsafe `any` access).
- Drop deprecated `setDynamicTooltip`; cleaner filename-char regex.
- `eslint-plugin-obsidianmd`: 0 errors, 1 informational warning (settings-search API).
- minAppVersion bumped to 1.8.7 (for `getLanguage`).

releases.author: slow-coding
releases.description:
First release — LLM Auto Title for Obsidian.

Generate note titles via a local LLM (LMStudio by default; any OpenAI-compat server such as Ollama / vLLM).

- Hotkey (default Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+T): title the current note
- Command: scan & title all timestamp notes
- Thinking off by default (`reasoning_effort:none`) — fast and stable; toggleable
- Configurable timestamp format / regex, scan folders, System prompt
- UI auto-localizes (English / 中文)

**Install:** copy `main.js` and `manifest.json` into `<vault>/.obsidian/plugins/llm-auto-title/`, then enable under Settings → Community plugins.
